title: mcp: working npm run test, README trim, and wire into gen /docs
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Three small things on the MCP package.

### 1. `npm run test` works now
`packages/mcp/package.json` referenced `test-mcp-client.js` and a `tests/integration/` vitest suite â€” neither existed.

- Adds `packages/mcp/test-mcp-client.js`: spawns the server over stdio, lists tools, sets a key, and runs a small live slice (`generateText`, `generateImageUrl`, `getBalance`). Skips authenticated calls when `POLLINATIONS_API_KEY` is unset.
- Drops the `test:integration` script + the unused `vitest` devDep.

Verified empirically end-to-end against `gen.pollinations.ai` with an `sk_` key â€” 8/8 pass (real model output, real balance, real URL, real perplexity answer).

### 2. README trim
- Drops the v1/v2 "What's New" + "Migration from v1.x" sections (changelog noise on a public docs page).
- Swaps hardcoded model/voice enums for "call `listX` for the live list" â€” the README itself already says everything is validated against the registry, so listing names just rots.
- ~50 lines lighter, evergreen.

### 3. Wired into `gen.pollinations.ai/docs`
Same pattern as CLI and BYOP â€” `import MCP_README from "../../../packages/mcp/README.md?raw"`, strip the H1, expose as:
- `## MCP Server` block in `/docs/llm.txt`
- `đź”Ś MCP Server` Scalar tag on the rendered `/docs` page

Added a smoke test that asserts the MCP section lands in the rendered LLM doc.

gist.headline: MCP docs join the hive
gist.blurb: Our Model Context Protocol (MCP) server documentation is now officially wired into the main API docs. We also cleaned up the README and added a proper end-to-end test script for contributors.
gist.summary: Wired the MCP server documentation into the main gen.pollinations.ai/docs endpoint and Scalar UI, removed hardcoded model lists from the README, and added a working stdio test script.
gist.impact: Developers will now see the MCP server instructions right alongside the rest of the API documentation, and contributors can reliably test the package locally with `npm run test`.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing documents and plugging a wire into a main server, while the Monitor Robot happily reads a freshly printed manual.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing tiny reading glasses, plugging a glowing wire into a retro server rack. Nearby, a chunky monitor-headed robot happily reads a pixelated instruction manual. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
